values: Difficult

evidence.quote_or_summary: Difficult

values: Suitable for Experienced Trekkers

evidence.quote_or_summary: Suitable for Experienced Trekkers

values: Overhanging narrow trail for about 1 km overlooking the Dhak valley when approaching Tali from Gorson Bugyal
Boulder sections present every day
Last 4-5 hours of summit day on snow over big boulders
Ridge section composed of 6 small ridge sections with ascending and descending portions requiring careful footing
Narrow and windy ridge near the summit

evidence.quote_or_summary: While approaching Tali from Gorson Bugyal, you trek on an overhanging narrow trail for about 1 km overlooking the Dhak valley.
Every day will include ascents, descents and boulder sections.
the last 4-5 hours of the trek will be on the snow over big boulders.
The ridge section can be seen as 6 small ridge sections put together– some ascending and others descending. Navigating these can be challenging, as ridges demand careful footing in both directions.
As you near the summit, the ridge becomes narrow and windy as well.

values: Summit day is very challenging both distance-wise and elevation-wise
Ascent of about 3,268 ft on the first day in an 8 km stretch
Climb of around 4,059 ft on the summit day to a maximum altitude of 15,069 ft
Total trek covers around 35 km in five trekking days with approximately 7,284 ft of elevation gain (7,785 ft to 15,069 ft)

evidence.quote_or_summary: The summit day of this trek is very challenging, both distance-wise and elevation-wise.
The ascent of about 3,268 ft on the first day of the trek in an 8 km stretch.
You climb around 4,059 ft on the summit day and reach a maximum altitude of 15,069 ft.
You will cover a total of around 35 km in five trekking days, during which you will gain approximately 7,284 ft (7,785 ft to 15,069 ft).

values: Narrow windy ridge near summit may require a rope, decided by the technical guide
Towards end of April, hard and icy snow requires route preparation with an ice axe by the technical guide
Indiahikes provides technical gear such as microspikes, gaiters, helmets, ropes wherever required

evidence.quote_or_summary: This section might require a rope. Your technical guide will take a call on this and prepare the trail for you.
Towards the end of April, the snow can become hard and icy. This again will require the technical guide to prepare the route for you with an ice axe.
our Trek Leaders and Technical Guides will provide you with all necessary guidance, support, and technical gear such as microspikes, gaiters, helmets, ropes, etc. wherever required.

values: Considerable elevation gain on the first day can make trekkers more prone to AMS
Maximum altitude reached is 15,069 ft on the summit day
Indiahikes team is equipped with high-altitude medical kits, oxygen cylinders, and stretchers to handle altitude-related emergencies

evidence.quote_or_summary: Since you gain considerable elevation on the very first day, it can make you more prone to AMS.
You climb around 4,059 ft on the summit day and reach a maximum altitude of 15,069 ft.
The Indiahikes team is equipped with high-altitude medical kits, oxygen cylinders, and stretchers to handle any altitude-related emergencies.

values: Rainfall or snowfall lasting for hours can change the entire trek dynamic
Rain can flood trails or make them too slushy to walk safely on
Snowfall can obstruct previously made paths, requiring fresh tracks to be cut
Summit day becomes even more challenging if it snows through the day
Extremely cold conditions on the summit climb along the exposed ridge can result in cold-related injuries
Khullara campsite is one of the coldest campsites among all Indiahikes treks, requiring appropriate layering
Overhanging narrow trail near Tali can get tricky on rainy days
Technical team and Trek Leader monitor weather and may turn back from any campsite if conditions deteriorate

evidence.quote_or_summary: Rainfall or snowfall lasting for hours can change the entire trek dynamic.
Rain can flood the trails or make them too slushy to walk safely on.
Snowfall can obstruct previously made paths, requiring fresh tracks to be cut in the snow.
Your summit day becomes even more challenging if it snows through the day.
The trek also gets extremely cold on the summit climb as you go up the exposed ridge. This can result in cold-related injuries.
The Khullara campsite is one of the coldest campsites among all the treks that we run at Indiahikes. Appropriate layering is extremely important here.
On rainy days, this stretch can get tricky.
If the weather deteriorates, the team may need to turn back from any campsite to ensure the safety of all trekkers.

values: Closest exit from Khullara and Tali campsites is via Dhak
Closest exit from the Gorson Bugyal side (before the overhanging section) is Auli
Closest Primary Healthcare Center is in Joshimath, 20 km by road from Karchi
For serious concerns, Dehradun is the best option, a 7-8 hour drive from both Karchi and Auli
Indiahikes team is equipped with high-altitude medical kits, oxygen cylinders, and stretchers
Trekkers must inform the Trek Leader about any pre-existing medical conditions or health concerns

evidence.quote_or_summary: If you are in the valley where Khullara and Tali campsites are located, the closest exit is via Dhak.
If you have not crossed the overhanging section of the trail and are still on the Gorson Bugyal side, the closest exit is Auli.
The closest Primary Healthcare Center is in Joshimath, just 20 kms by road from Karchi.
for any serious concern, heading to Dehradun is the best option. Dehradun is a 7-8 hr drive from both Karchi and Auli.
The Indiahikes team is equipped with high-altitude medical kits, oxygen cylinders, and stretchers to handle any altitude-related emergencies.
Please inform your Trek Leader about any pre-existing medical conditions or health concerns, no matter how minor they may seem.

values: Several difficult sections require trekkers to be alert and trek carefully
Safety is a shared responsibility requiring active participation and attention to safety protocols from trekkers

evidence.quote_or_summary: There are several difficult sections on this trek that require you to be alert and trek carefully.
we believe that safety is a shared responsibility. We need your active participation and attention to safety protocols to ensure a safe and successful trek.
